entities.description: As a subsection of 'Experimental Details' within the 'BookRAG' paper, this section defines the Retrieval Recall metric used to evaluate retrieval quality based on parsed PDF block granularity (paragraphs, tables, images).
The specific aspect of system performance being measured in this section, assessed via the granularity of retrieved blocks.
The fundamental units of data (paragraphs, tables, images) from which ground-truth and retrieved sets are constructed for evaluation.
The input query variable used to define the set of required ground-truth blocks.
The set of manually labeled ground-truth blocks required to answer a given query.
The set of unique blocks retrieved by the system for a given query.
The specific mathematical formula defined in this section to calculate retrieval recall, handling parsing errors.

relations.description: Retrieval Quality is the primary concept evaluated within section A.1.3.
PDF Blocks serve as the granular units of analysis for the evaluation described in section A.1.3.
The variable 'Query q' is a key parameter defined in the context of section A.1.3.
The variable 'B_gold' represents the ground truth set utilized in the definition provided in section A.1.3.
The variable 'B_ret' represents the retrieved set utilized in the definition provided in section A.1.3.
The metric 'Recall_ret' is the central formula and subject explicitly defined in section A.1.3.
